mnemonics.title: SAD-C Model Selection
mnemonics.content: Sequence (time-order), Activity (workflow), Deployment (hardware), Class (structure), Collaboration (organization) - Each serves different purpose to supplement use cases.

mnemonics.title: BEC - Boundary Entity Control
mnemonics.content: Remember the three object types in sequence diagrams: BEC - Boundary (interface), Entity (data), Control (coordinate).

mnemonics.title: ACS - Activity Control State
mnemonics.content: ACS helps remember: Activity for workflows, Control objects in sequences, State for object behavior changes.

acronyms.usage: Standard language for visualizing, specifying, constructing, and documenting software systems
Process of analyzing and designing system using models like UML
Cards used to identify classes and their responsibilities during design

visualTips.visualization: Think of sequence diagram as a timeline going left to right - objects on top, time flowing downward, messages as horizontal arrows connecting lifelines.
Activity diagrams are like flowcharts - activities as rounded rectangles, decisions as diamonds, arrows showing flow direction, swimlanes as lanes in a pool.
State diagrams are like state transition tables visualized - circles for states, arrows for transitions labeled with events that cause them.

analogies.analogy: Use case is like a movie poster showing what's in the movie. Supplementary models are like the full screenplay showing how the story unfolds.
Sequence diagrams are like movie scripts - they show actors (objects), when they speak (messages), and in what order.
Activity diagrams are like cooking recipes - step-by-step instructions with decisions (add salt if...), parallel tasks (chop vegetables while boiling water).
